subject:
Re: M stuck at "Precomputing Gradient Weight" + Warp 1.1.0 link
 broken
body:
Here is the warp 1.10 beta version that was uploaded then! 

https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/tp17eqypvj44bt9xl202h/Warp_110_beta1.zip?rlkey=rp81pb9zkcq6u9x8f0upp622j&dl=0


best, 
Jake

On Friday, September 15, 2023 at 7:20:16 AM UTC-4 B Wimmer wrote:

> Hi all, 
>
> Somehow I previously overlooked this thread, 
> <https://groups.google.com/g/warp-em/c/nIi7AF0dVmA/m/7aa_QaT9BAAJ> which 
> details (presumably) the same issue; *unfortunately the Download page for 
> the Nightly builds which are supposed to fix this is also broken *- any 
> help getting the binaries with the fixes is much appreciated. 
>
> Sorry for double-texting,
> Benedikt
>
> On Friday, September 15, 2023 at 12:04:13 PM UTC+2 B Wimmer wrote:
>
>> Dear Dimitry, Dear Mailing List, 
>>
>> I am trying out M on a FIB dataset. Subtomograms reconstructed in Warp -> 
>> Relion 3.1.4 -> M.
>> As a first step, I am running a Geometry refinement. After a processing a 
>> few tiltseries without issues, it stops at *one specific tiltseries* 
>> (tried 3 times with clearing temp folder). The M GUI is stuck at the 
>> "Precomputing Gradient Weight" step. The M worker crashes. 
>> Worker_0_crash.txt reads as follows:
>>
>> "System.Exception: cudaErrorMemoryAllocation
>>    at Warp.GPU.CheckGPUExceptions() in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\GPU.cs:line 
>> 1175
>>    at WarpWorker.Program.Main(String[] args) in 
>> D:\Dev\warp2\WarpWorker\Program.cs:line 322"
>>
>> Machine is running Windows Server 2019 Datacenter, 1 TB RAM, RTX6000 GPU 
>> (24GB VRAM), Warp/M 1.0.9.
>>
>> On a related note, I tried to download the 1.1.0 beta that was posted to 
>> this list about a year ago, but the Dropbox link seems broken, as are the 
>> links to the nightly versions on the main website. Is there an alternate 
>> link somewhere?
>>
>> Appreciate all insights - best,
>> Benedikt
>>
>
